reasoning: The most recent value of the Cleveland Fed's 1-year expected inflation rate is 2.887 for April 2025. This value is relatively high compared to the data over the last 13 months, during which only one prior month (May 2024 at 2.935) exceeded it. The FRED statistical tool gives a 30% probability of an increase by late May 2025 based on trend and seasonal factors, which serves as a highly reliable primary anchor. In the summer and fall of 2024, inflation expectations steadily decreased, which suggests downward pressure for the July and October 2025 horizons. However, given the longer forecast horizon, uncertainty is higher for those later dates, so predictions are drawn closer to 0.5 according to instructions. This results in probability estimates of 0.30, 0.38, and 0.45 for the three respective resolution dates.
